name: Chiggi Stats
description: Detailed playback statistics and reporting for Jellyfin. Tracks what every user is watching, how long they watch, and provides a filterable dashboard accessible directly inside Jellyfin. Supports filtering by user, date range, and media type. Data is stored in a local SQLite database with configurable retention.
overview: Detailed playback statistics with user/date/media filters.

versions.changelog: Fix: stats dashboard was completely empty — Jellyfin serializes API responses as PascalCase but stats.js was reading camelCase property names (e.g. summary.totalSessions instead of summary.TotalSessions), causing every value to be undefined and all sections to render empty.

Fix: endDate filter now covers the full selected day — bare date strings from the UI were parsed as midnight UTC, causing all same-day playback events to be excluded from every query and the stats dashboard to appear empty.

Diag: add info-level log at the very first line of OnPlaybackStart (before all null-checks) so Jellyfin logs confirm whether ISessionManager.PlaybackStart fires at all — needed to diagnose why no playback events are recorded after the v1.0.10 session-fallback fix.

Fix: PlaybackTracker now falls back to args.Session.UserId/UserName when args.Users is empty — in Jellyfin 10.11 the Users list on playback events can be empty, causing all sessions to be silently dropped and no data recorded. Add info-level logging on each tracked start/stop event so Jellyfin logs confirm recording is working.

Fix: remove is="emby-button" from Apply, Reset, Prev, and Next buttons — emby-button component styles were overriding page CSS making them appear grey. Add explicit cs-btn / cs-btn-primary CSS classes for consistent button appearance.

Fix: replace [Authorize(Policy = "DefaultAuthorization")] with [Authorize] on activity and summary endpoints — Jellyfin 10.11 does not register the 'DefaultAuthorization' named policy, causing InvalidOperationException before the handler ran. Fix: inject IAuthorizationContext so user resolution uses Jellyfin's own auth pipeline rather than hardcoded claim names. Fix: switch loadOverview() from Promise.all to Promise.allSettled so summary data renders even if the admin overview call fails.

Fix: inject IAuthorizationContext into StatsController so user resolution uses Jellyfin's own auth pipeline rather than hardcoded claim names — claim-only resolution failed silently in Jellyfin 10.11 causing all data endpoints to return 401. Fix: switch loadOverview() from Promise.all to Promise.allSettled so summary data renders even when the admin-only reports/overview call fails (e.g. auth edge case or non-admin session).

Fix: GetAuthenticatedUserId() now checks 'Jellyfin-UserId' claim first (Jellyfin 10.11 InternalClaimTypes.UserId) — wrong claim name was causing all data endpoints to return 401 and show 'Failed to load data.'. Fix: remove is="emby-button" from tab buttons so page CSS fully controls their appearance — emby-button component CSS was overriding inactive tab button background to grey regardless of !important.

Fix: replace raw fetch() with ApiClient.ajax() so Jellyfin's full MediaBrowser auth header is sent — token-only header was rejected causing all API calls to fail. Fix: add !important to active tab button CSS to override emby-button component styles that were making all tabs appear grey.

Fix: delete entire runtimes/ directory from plugin zip after flattening native libs. Jellyfin's plugin scanner crawls ALL subdirectories recursively for *.dll — any native DLL anywhere in the tree disables the plugin. runtimes/win-*/native/e_sqlite3.dll was causing BadImageFormatException even when not at the root.

Fix: exclude Windows native e_sqlite3.dll from plugin root — Jellyfin's plugin scanner tried to load it as a managed assembly causing BadImageFormatException on startup. Only Linux/macOS native libs are now flattened to the plugin directory.

Feat: full reporting dashboard with Overview, Playback, Movies, Series, Seasons, Episodes, Music, Box Sets, Users, and Devices tabs. Admin-only inventory reports backed by live Jellyfin library data. Paginated playback history with date/user/media-type filters. Pure CSS bar chart — no external CDN dependencies.
Fix: SQLite native library now found correctly in Jellyfin plugin context (no more Malfunctioned). Fix: database initialisation split into separate statements to prevent startup crash.
Initial release. Playback tracking with SQLite storage, activity log integration, and in-Jellyfin stats dashboard.

name: Trakt
description: Sync your Jellyfin library to trakt.tv and scrobble your watch status. This is a fixed fork of the official Jellyfin Trakt plugin addressing 409 Conflict errors, rate-limit handling, duplicate watch history entries, TV show scrobbling failures in Jellyfin 10.11, and more.
overview: Sync library and scrobble to trakt.tv — fixed fork with bug fixes.

versions.changelog: Fix: handle HTTP 420 Account Limit Exceeded from Trakt gracefully — free accounts are capped at 100 collection items (enforced Jan 2025). Previously this threw an exception and aborted the entire sync. Now logs a clear warning and continues. Disable collection sync in Trakt settings or upgrade to Trakt VIP to resolve.

Fix: TraktMetadata.Is3D changed from bool to bool? — Trakt API returns null for the 3d field on some collection items, causing JsonException on sync. Lifted bool? != bool comparison in Extensions.cs remains valid and correct.

Fix: remove explicit Microsoft.Extensions.Http 10.0.1 package reference — it is a .NET 10 package that pulled in Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.Abstractions v10 as a bundled dependency. At runtime Jellyfin provides .NET 9 versions of these assemblies, causing IServiceCollection type identity mismatch in RegisterServices, making the plugin fail with NotSupported. Microsoft.Extensions.Http is already supplied by the FrameworkReference to Microsoft.AspNetCore.App.

Fix: add ExcludeAssets=runtime to Jellyfin package references — without it, Jellyfin's own assemblies (MediaBrowser.Controller, MediaBrowser.Model, Jellyfin.Data, etc.) and all transitive deps (EFCore, Polly, ICU4N, etc.) were bundled into the plugin zip. Jellyfin's PluginLoadContext loaded duplicate instances of these assemblies, causing IHasWebPages type identity check to fail silently — plugin loaded but settings page never appeared.
Fix: restored Trakt settings/configuration page button (missing RootNamespace caused embedded resource path mismatch).
Fork of official v27 with fixes: #265 rate-limit HasFlag bug, #266 409 Conflict crash, #226 duplicate history (HashSet dedup), #258 null Series on TV scrobble.
